title: osmanthus heterophyllus
intro: Osmanthus heterophyllus, also known as the tea olive or false holly, is a species of flowering plant native to Japan and Korea. The plant is known for its fragrant flowers, leaves, and berries, which have been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The leaves and flowers of Osmanthus heterophyllus contain compounds with medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its use in treating various health conditions. Osmanthus heterophyllus has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and capsules to promote relaxation, improve sleep, and boost the immune system.
